failures.explanation: UBP15 OE has limited propagation in algebraic mode (single-output, only Integument_Growth). ODE/RWR resolve correctly with stronger Hill saturation. Algebraic-only failure due to weak signal at gm=2.0 against geometric mean dilution.

ubp15 da1 double — UBP15 is epistatic to DA1 (UBP15 KO suppresses da1-1 enlargement back toward smaller seeds). Network captures DA1 KO effect (enlarged) but cannot model the epistatic suppression because UBP15 only routes to Integument_Growth(+).

Algebraic mode shows minimal phenotype movement on KLUH OE (gm=2.0). ODE/RWR resolve correctly. KLUH→Integument_Growth single edge with geometric mean dilution at the 16-input hub absorbs the increase.
failures.fix_strategy: Increase KLUH-driven signal weight or trim Integument_Growth inputs (currently 16, well above 7-cap).

failures.explanation: CYP78A9 single KO mapped to KLUH gm=0.997 (composite-member encoding per Trap 2). Algebraic and ODE both predict unchanged because composite gm=0.997 produces ratio ~1.0. Biology shows mild seed-size reduction.
failures.fix_strategy: If CYP78A6/A8/A9 should each have observable single-KO effect, split composite into individual paralog nodes (would also need new curated edges per paralog).

failures.explanation: Same as T012 — CYP78A6 mapped to KLUH composite gm=0.997.
failures.fix_strategy: Split CYP78A composite or accept as known limitation.

failures.explanation: Double KO of two CYP78A paralogs mapped to KLUH gm=0.5; algebraic predicts ratio ~0.98 (just outside threshold). ODE catches as unchanged.
failures.fix_strategy: Lower mapped gm to 0.3 for double-paralog KO.

failures.explanation: Single DELLA-family member (gaiT6) KO mapped to DELLA gm=0.997 per Trap 2. Composite predicts unchanged but biology shows mild seed-size reduction.
Same as T026 — single RGL1 KO mapped to DELLA composite gm=0.997.
Same as T026 — single RGL2 KO mapped to DELLA composite gm=0.997.
IKU2 OE algebraic ratio 1.028 — just below the 0.05 threshold. ODE/RWR catch as increased.
failures.fix_strategy: Algebraic-only borderline; ODE/RWR resolve correctly.

failures.explanation: RAV1 OE represses ABI5/MINI3/IKU2 (all in network). Network: ABI5 down → less Seed_Size inhibition → predicted INCREASED. But biology says decreased. Indicates the RAV1→ABI5(-) edge sign or RAV1's net seed-size effect may be more nuanced (RAV1 represses positive seed-size genes too).
failures.fix_strategy: Consider adding RAV1→Integument_Growth(-) or splitting RAV1 effects across multiple targets so the net seed-size effect aligns with biology.

failures.explanation: Same RAV1 logic in reverse — RAV1 KO releases ABI5 inhibition → ABI5 up → seed_size predicted decreased. Biology: minimal change. Ratio is extreme (~0.14) due to bounded inverse explosion.
failures.fix_strategy: Re-examine RAV1 wiring; possibly remove RAV1→ABI5(-) or balance with positive RAV1 targets.

failures.explanation: LAC2 not in network so test reduces to MINI3 KO with baseline=mutant. Without LAC2 contribution, no rescue effect can be modeled.
failures.fix_strategy: Step 1 LITERATURE REVIEW would need to add LAC2 with curated downstream edges.

failures.explanation: ckx3 ckx5 → CK accumulates → Cytokinin→Endosperm_Proliferation(-1) drives Endosperm DOWN, predicting decreased seed. Biology shows increased seed (ovule-side CK promotion dominates). Network has Cytokinin→Ovule_size(+) but the negative endosperm effect outweighs.
failures.fix_strategy: Re-evaluate balance between Cytokinin→Endosperm(-) and Cytokinin→Ovule(+); or add Cytokinin→Integument_Growth(+) to capture maternal CK promotion of seed growth.

failures.explanation: Same as T054 — triple removes additional CK suppressor AHP6, even higher CK level, same negative effect on Endosperm_Prolif dominates.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T054.

failures.explanation: BR-deficient det2 should give smaller seeds. Network: BR low → BRI1 low → BIN2 high → BZR1 low → RAV1 high (BZR1⊣RAV1) → RAV1 represses ABI5 → ABI5 low → less seed_size inhibition → INCREASED predicted. The BR cascade inversion through RAV1⊣ABI5 produces wrong direction.
failures.fix_strategy: Add direct BZR1→Integument_Growth(+) or BR→growth-positive edge so that BR-deficient mutants don't rely on the RAV1⊣ABI5 detour.

failures.explanation: Same as T059 — BR biosynthesis KO produces wrong direction via RAV1⊣ABI5 detour.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T059.

failures.explanation: Same as T059 — BR receptor KO produces wrong direction via the same RAV1⊣ABI5 inversion.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T059 — rebalance BR-Seed_Size cascade so BR deficiency reliably produces decreased.

failures.explanation: bzr1-1D dominant constitutive BZR1; paper reports unchanged seed size despite gain-of-function. Network with BZR1 gm=2.0 predicts increased due to broad downstream activation. Likely paper-specific allele behavior.
Rescue test (baseline=det2): AP2 KO suppresses det2 small-seed phenotype back toward larger. Without strong BR-cascade-to-growth coupling (see T059-T061), the rescue can't be captured.
failures.fix_strategy: Same fix as T059 — strengthen BR pathway; also depends on AP2⊣Integument_Growth signal.

failures.explanation: Same epistasis structure as T063 — ARF2 KO rescues det2 small seed.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T063.

failures.explanation: 35S:MINI3 in det2 background — MINI3 OE rescues det2 small seed. Same root cause as T063/T064: weak BR-Seed_Size coupling means rescue can't propagate.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T063/T064.

failures.explanation: abi3 KO causes maturation defects → smaller, mishapen seeds. Network: ABI3 KO → ABI5 drops (ABI5 needs ABA+ABI4 still) → ABI5⊣Seed_Size released → predicted INCREASED. The maturation/storage program is missing from the network (Storage_reserves was removed in iter 3 trim) so ABI3's loss-of-storage effect on seed mass cannot be captured.
failures.fix_strategy: Add LAFL→Storage_reserves→Seed_Size sub-cascade back into network (re-introduce Storage_reserves node) OR add direct LAFL→Seed_Size(+) edges.

failures.explanation: Same as T072 — fus3 KO → maturation defect → biology says decreased; network predicts increased via released ABI5 inhibition. Same root cause: missing storage/maturation arm.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T072.

failures.explanation: Same as T072/T073 — lec1 KO → maturation defect.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T072 — restore LAFL→storage→Seed_Size.

failures.explanation: Same as T072-T074 — lec2 KO → maturation defect; missing storage cascade.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T072.

failures.explanation: GA application increases seed size in eLife 2016 ovule study. Network applies Gomez 2023 logic where DELLA POSITIVELY regulates seed size (DELLA→ANT(+)). High GA → low DELLA → low ANT → smaller seed predicted. Two papers report opposite directions for GA effect; network adopted Gomez framework.
failures.fix_strategy: Resolving requires choosing between two contradictory papers; current Gomez 2023 framework is more recent and direct.

failures.explanation: Brz inhibits BR biosynthesis → same root cause as T059-T061 (BR deficiency producing wrong direction via RAV1⊣ABI5 detour).
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T059 — strengthen BR pathway.

failures.explanation: gUBP15-GFP in da1-1 — both DA1 KO and UBP15 OE should enlarge seeds. Network DA1 KO already drives Integument_Growth high; UBP15 OE adds more but signal saturates against the geometric mean dilution at the 16-input hub.
failures.fix_strategy: Trim Integument_Growth inputs to allow signal headroom.

failures.explanation: Endosperm-specific bacterial auxin synthesis causes ECTOPIC high auxin in endosperm tissue, producing seed defects. Network sees Auxin OE globally and predicts increased (auxin promotes Integument_Growth, Endosperm_Prolif). Cannot distinguish tissue-localised vs systemic auxin.
failures.fix_strategy: Tissue-compartment modeling outside framework scope.

failures.explanation: ttg2-6/+ heterozygous mapped as TTG2 gm=0.5; algebraic predicts ratio ~0.98 (within threshold). ODE/RWR catch correctly.
failures.fix_strategy: Algebraic-only borderline.

failures.explanation: val1 val2 KO derepresses LAFL TFs (LEC1/2/FUS3/ABI3) → ABI5 up (via ABI3→ABI5) → ABI5⊣Seed_Size strong → predicted DECREASED. But biology shows ectopic embryo identity in seedlings (not necessarily larger seeds — phenotype is mostly post-germination). Direction encoded as 'increased' may itself be questionable for seed-size-specific assay.
failures.fix_strategy: May reflect a test-encoding error rather than network failure; consider re-checking expected_direction.

failures.explanation: Same root cause as T111 — pkl KO derepresses LAFL → ABI5 up → seed size predicted decreased.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T111.

failures.explanation: abi4 KO has minimal observed effect on seed size in literature. Network: ABI4 KO → ABI3, ABI5 both drop → strong release of ABI5⊣Seed_Size inhibition → ratio explodes to ~15. Network amplifies a mild biological effect.
failures.fix_strategy: Reduce ABI5⊣Seed_Size weight or split into multiple smaller-effect inhibitors; alternatively add positive ABI4 outputs to phenotype to balance.

failures.explanation: Same root cause as T054/T055 — exogenous CK drives Endosperm_Prolif down via Cytokinin→Endosperm(-1) edge dominance. Algebraic-only failure; ODE/RWR resolve correctly.
failures.fix_strategy: Same as T054.

primary_root_causes: BR cascade weakly coupled to seed-size phenotype: BR-deficient mutants (det2, dwf4, bri1) produce wrong direction via RAV1⊣ABI5 detour rather than direct growth promotion. Affects T059, T060, T061, T101, and rescue tests T063-T065.

Maturation/storage program missing: removal of Storage_reserves in BUILDER iteration 3 left ABI5 as the dominant downstream effector for the LAFL cascade. Now KO of any LAFL TF (ABI3/FUS3/LEC1/LEC2) releases ABI5⊣Seed_Size and predicts increased instead of decreased. Affects T072-T075, T118, and contributes to T111-T112.
Cytokinin→Endosperm(-) edge dominates over Cytokinin→Ovule(+) for net seed-size effect, opposite to biology. Affects T054, T055, T121.
Composite-paralog single-member KOs predict unchanged by design (gm=0.997). Some single-member KOs do produce mild effects in literature. Affects T012, T014, T026-T028.
